member.office_address: 1213 Longworth House Office Building Washington DC 20515-2003

form_analysis.form_header_text: FY 2027 Programmatic and Language Appropriations Submission Form
form_analysis.form_instructions_text:
This form is to submit Programmatic and Language Requests for the FY 2027 Budget.

For more information about Appropriations, please visit our website at https://elfreth.house.gov/services/appropriations-requests.

NOTE: OUR FORM DEADLINE IS MARCH 4th. 

If you have any questions, please contact our office at 202-225-4016 or email our staff for further assistance before submitting a request.

sections.description: Details for the requesting organization and their primary points of contact.
Specific information regarding the programmatic or language appropriation request.

fields.label: Connection to MD-03 or State of Maryland
fields.help_text: Please explain your organization's presence or impact within Maryland's 3rd district or the state.
fields.draft_value:
* NASA Science obligated over $1.32 billion to Maryland's 3rd District between FY 2023 and FY 2025.
* The Johns Hopkins University Applied Physics Laboratory (APL) in Laurel is a cornerstone of the district, managing the $1.1 billion Dragonfly rotorcraft mission to Titan.
* Universities Space Research Association (USRA) in Columbia manages over $40 million in active grants for planetary science support and national STEM education initiatives.
* MD-03 institutions drive critical research in Earth magnetic imaging, lunar surface exploration, and Martian gamma-ray detection.
fields.rationale: Cites specific total obligations ($1.32B) and the two largest recipients in MD-03 (JHU APL and USRA) along with their primary mission activities (Dragonfly, STEM education) as detailed in the local spending data.

fields.label: Type of Request
fields.rationale: This is a request for a specific funding level ($9 billion) for an existing NASA program (Science Mission Directorate), which is classified as a programmatic request in the reference values.

fields.label: Name of Subcommittee
fields.help_text: Select the relevant House Appropriations Subcommittee.
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration
Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
Energy & Water Development, and Related Agencies
Labor, Health and Human Services, Education, and Related Agencies
Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies
National Security, Department of State, and Related Programs (Formerly: State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs)
Transportation, Housing & Urban Development, and Related Agencies
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: NASA funding, including the Science Mission Directorate, is jurisdictional to the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies Subcommittee as specified in the reference values.

fields.label: Proposal Title
fields.draft_value: NASA Science Mission Directorate Funding Request
fields.rationale: Standard programmatic request title referencing the Science Mission Directorate as specified in the reference values.

fields.label: Short Description
fields.draft_value:
* Programmatic request of $9 billion for NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD).
* Sustains robust funding for Earth Science, Planetary Science, Astrophysics, Heliophysics, and Biological and Physical Sciences.
* Ensures continued development of high-priority missions identified by the National Academies' Decadal Surveys.
fields.rationale: Based on the provided Funding Ask ($9B) and the standard description of SMD's component disciplines and programmatic nature.

fields.label: Justification for Request
fields.draft_value:
* Economic Engine: Every $1 invested in NASA returns $3 to the economy, supporting a specialized high-tech workforce at institutions like JHU APL and USRA.
* Global Leadership: Maintains U.S. preeminence in space science as China rapidly expands its lunar and planetary exploration capabilities.
* Workforce Stability: Funding provides the certainty needed to retain world-class scientists and engineers following recent budget disruptions.
* Scientific Priority: Supports a rigorous, peer-reviewed roadmap for discovery, from weather forecasting to planetary defense and deep-space exploration.
fields.rationale: Incorporates strategic advocacy talking points regarding ROI, geopolitical competition with China, workforce retention, and the scientific authority of Decadal Surveys.

fields.label: MD03 Connection (if applicable)
fields.draft_value: NASA Science has invested over $1.3 billion in MD-03 since FY2023, supporting key institutions like Johns Hopkins APL in Laurel and USRA in Columbia on critical missions including the Dragonfly rotorcraft for Titan exploration.
fields.rationale: Directly references the $1,326,240,499.23 in total district obligations and specific high-value projects at JHU APL and USRA identified in the NASA spending context.

fields.label: FY26 Enacted Level (if applicable)
fields.help_text: Provide the enacted funding amount for the previous fiscal year.
fields.rationale: Uses the authoritative enacted amount for FY2026 provided in the reference values.

fields.label: FY27 President's Budget Number
fields.help_text: Funding level proposed in the President's FY27 budget request.
fields.rationale: The reference values state that the FY2027 President's Budget Request has not yet been released.

fields.label: FY27 Requested Amount
fields.help_text: The specific dollar amount requested for the current cycle.
fields.rationale: Uses the specific $9 billion request amount for NASA SMD as established in the funding ask context.

fields.label: Language Text
fields.rationale: The request context specifies that no bill or report language is being requested for this programmatic funding ask.

fields.label: Is there a Dear Colleague letter circulating on this request? If yes, which member is leading?
fields.draft_value: Yes, led by Representatives Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA).
fields.rationale: Based on the provided house letter leads in the congressional support section of the reference values.

fields.label: Other Members Receiving Request
fields.help_text: List other Congressional offices to which this request has been submitted.
fields.draft_value: Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading an existing multi-member House letter in support of this request. Request also being submitted to additional House offices.
fields.rationale: Request is being submitted to multiple offices; Bacon/Chu lead the House letter per reference values.

fields.label: Please upload and documentation, letter, or other helpful information for your request here

top_grants.description: The National Aeronautics and Space Association (NASA) supports the president and the National science and technology council committee on STEM educat
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Supporting national STEM education policy development for federal leadership.

top_grants.description: The Universities Space Research Association (USRA)-Lunar and Planetary Institute (LPI) proposes to plan and implement annual meetings and provide travel and salary support for NASA S program analysis (or assessment) groups (PAGS). The eight Planetary
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Planning and supporting annual reviews for NASA planetary science programs.

top_grants.description: The Universities Space Research Association (USRA) acting by and through the lunar and Planetary institute (referred to throughout the document as the institute or lpi) is proposing a Science collaboration to facilitate the efforts of the Astromaterials Research and Exploration Science (ARES) division by providing scientific analysis and expertise to complement and extend the existing capabilities in ares. As a top quality  world renowned Science institute  the lpi is uniquely positioned to provide technical personnel with strong Research credentials that would provide a forum for developing Research ideas in lunar and Planetary Science. Lpi S experience in dealing with NASA badging and visas for foreign nationals brings a unique resource to the ares division. Additionally  the lpi will provide use of the facilities at the institute  including  but not limited to  meeting facilities outside the JSC fence to facilitate conversations with collaborators; it resources and staff that provide complementary resources to those at JSC; a lunar and Planetary Science library collection that exceeds 62 000 books  journals  and archived NASA documents; the entire apollo Image collection housed at the institute S Regional Planetary Image Facility (RPIF); and access to scholars and students to address key Planetary Science questions.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Providing expert scientific support and unique resources for planetary science.
